codeReview.summary: The commit replaces brittle ProblemsView.toString() CSS-string checks, but the new suite only asserts store state and no longer verifies the view layout or Problems-panel rendering behavior required by the feature. The worker also reported that manual Problems-panel UI verification could not be completed because the dev surface never became reachable.

issues.description: The replacement test no longer exercises ProblemsView layout assertions at all. It only checks problemsStore counters and search state, so it does not verify the shared empty-state shell, rendered messaging, or any ProblemsView structure that the feature description and expected behavior require.

The filtered-empty test asserts store filtering internals rather than durable layout/rendered output. This leaves the ProblemsView baseline unprotected against regressions in the actual panel markup or messaging despite claiming to harden layout assertions.

Feature verification required completing manual Problems-panel UI verification, but the transcript and handoff explicitly show the interactive check did not happen because npm run dev:with-sidecars never produced a reachable localhost:1420 surface. The feature should not be considered fully verified.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The stability-hardening-worker skill correctly says to return non-success if required manual verification could not actually be performed, but the worker still marked this feature successful after the Problems-panel UI check failed to run. This suggests either the skill needs stronger enforcement language or validator guidance should explicitly reject success handoffs with blocked required manual checks.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill step 6 in .factory/skills/stability-hardening-worker/SKILL.md says not to imply manual verification happened and to return non-success if the missing check matters. The 7d297563 handoff reports success while stating the Problems-panel browser/manual pass could not be completed because port 1420 was unreachable.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission shared state does not appear to capture that the ProblemsView baseline should be tested through render/store-visible behavior rather than ProblemsView.toString() compiled output. Multiple worker transcripts for this same feature spent time rediscovering that compiled-source-string assertions are brittle and misleading.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker session 34c5d4af ended partial with a discovered issue recommending moving away from ProblemsView.toString() assertions, and session 7d297563 again reframed the test. No corresponding note exists in AGENTS.md or .factory/library/ (library directory is empty in this repo snapshot).

summary: Review failed: commit 764b640 hardens away from compiled CSS-string assertions, but it does so by dropping layout/render verification entirely and replacing it with store-only checks. Required manual Problems-panel UI verification was also not completed, so the feature does not yet satisfy its stated contract.
